title: synaphea petiolaris subsp. simplex
intro: Synaphea petiolaris subsp. simplex, also known as the Western Australian synaphea, is a shrub native to Western Australia with small, yellow flowers that bloom from January to April. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Synaphea petiolaris subsp. simplex include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and provide relief from various skin conditions. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves to harness its medicinal properties.
